steps.title: Identify the core assumption
steps.content: The student claims that small breeds must share a version of the gene that represses IGF-1. This assumes a direct link: one gene version is shared among small breeds and is responsible for their small body size.

steps.title: Find evidence that breaks this link
steps.content:
Choice D breaks the link: if some small breeds with low IGF-1 have a different gene version than toy poodles, then small body size doesn't require the student's assumed shared version. This directly contradicts the student's claim that a specific “repressive” version is responsible for small size.

The other options don't challenge this link: A and B discuss the gene's activity or consistency but don't show that different versions produce the same phenotype; C addresses mammalian size variation, unrelated to gene versions.

This matches choice D — D is correct.
